questions.question: Final pressure is higher than initial pressure of a container filled with an ideal gas at constant temperature. What will be the value of equilibrium constant ?
questions.explanation: An increase in pressure at constant temperature and volume indicates that the reaction proceeds with an increase in the number of gaseous moles ($\Delta n_g > 0$). An equilibrium constant $K < 1$ signifies that the equilibrium favors the reactants, meaning the reaction does not proceed extensively toward the products. Thus, the system reaches equilibrium with a higher proportion of reactants, which is consistent with a small equilibrium constant value.
